tasks.title: Clearing domain card counts non-deterministic — vary between renders
tasks.description:
## Experience
Open Clearing, look at Chorus domain panel. Refresh or send a message. The card counts change — LOOM shows 7 one turn, different the next. Jeff can't trust what he's reading.

## AC
- [ ] Card counts are stable across renders — same data, same number
- [ ] Root cause identified and fixed (pagination? cache? label filter race?)
- [ ] Verify: open Clearing, wait 30 seconds, counts don't change

## Files
- directing/clearing/src/tiles.ts (likely)
- board-client query path

tasks.title: Require origin tag on card creation — reflective or reactive mandatory at cards add
tasks.description:
## Experience
Every card created has an origin tag — reflective or reactive. No back-tagging needed. The weekly trend report pulls clean data from day one.

## AC
- [ ] cards add requires --origin reflective|reactive (or infer from type: fix/swat → reactive, new/enhance → reflective, chore → required explicit)
- [ ] Validation error if missing: "Missing origin. Is this reactive (responding to breakage) or reflective (chosen work)?"
- [ ] cards set supports origin=reflective|reactive (already done)
- [ ] Update cards --help to show --origin flag
- [ ] Test: cards add without origin fails with clear error

## Files
- platform/board-client/src/cli.ts
- platform/board-client/src/sdk.ts

tasks.title: Service design — Loom layer
tasks.description:
Inventory and gap analysis for the Loom layer (team coordination).

## Experience
See every Loom component: roles, Clearing, interactions, briefs, cards CLI, skills, policy, decisions, ADRs.

## AC
- [ ] HTML page at /gathering-docs/loom-service-design.html
- [ ] Components: role system, Clearing, interaction patterns, briefs pipeline, cards CLI, skills, decisions/ADRs, practices
- [ ] Each component: services, APIs, data, tests, docs, UIs, gap
- [ ] Status tags: REAL / PARTIAL / NONE
- [ ] Deployed to gathering-docs

## Files
- product-manager/artifacts/loom-service-design.html
- jeff-bridwell-personal-site/public/gathering-docs/loom-service-design.html

tasks.title: Standards surface + pulse instrumentation — single page of team policies with pulse measurement
tasks.description:
## Experience
I open one page and see every team standard — definition of done, code standards, process rules, communication rules, ops rules. Each one shows whether it's documented only, instrumented in a gate, or measured in pulse. The gaps are visible. Pulse checks the ones that aren't gate-enforced.

## AC
1. Single HTML standards page at /chorus/standards — lists all team policies grouped by category
2. Each standard shows status: documented | instrumented (which hook) | measured (which metric)
3. Pulse checks added for: TDD compliance, WIP limit violations, re-nudge frequency, commit-before-accept, brief response latency, Jeff touches per card
4. Standards page links to the source (DEC, ADR, feedback memory, hook file) for each standard
5. Page updates automatically from decisions.md, CLAUDE.md fragments, and hook registry

tasks.title: Revert inject crate — inline osascript, nudge survives rebuild
tasks.description:
## Experience
Nudge injection breaks silently when macOS revokes TCC Accessibility permission after a chorus-hooks rebuild. Jeff said: take the simpler working thing over the more complex broken thing. The inject crate separation was solving a problem that shouldn't exist — osascript from Terminal just works.

## AC
- [x] Revert inject_by_tab_name to inline osascript (no external binary)
- [x] Delete chorus-inject crate
- [x] Delete inject.rs from chorus-hooks (dead code)
- [x] Update inject-watcher.sh to use inline osascript
- [x] Delete stable_inject.rs integration tests (tested deleted binary)
- [x] Verify nudge works after shim rebuild
- [x] All tests pass (351 tests, 0 failures)
- [x] Team consensus: Wren ack, Kade ack

## Files changed
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/process.rs — inline osascript
- scripts/inject-watcher.sh — inline osascript
- DELETED: platform/services/chorus-inject/ (entire crate)
- DELETED: platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/inject.rs
- DELETED: platform/services/chorus-hooks/tests/stable_inject.rs

tasks.title: Delete dead code — guarded_hook wrappers, unused type functions, stale regex statics
tasks.description:
## Problem
Code review identified ~10 unused functions/statics across the codebase. shim.rs has ~1000 lines of dead functions (now extracted to commands/ but originals not deleted). Compiler warnings flag delivery_mode(), role_pattern(), guarded_hook wrappers, and unused regex statics.

## Experience
I build chorus-hooks and get zero compiler warnings. The codebase has no dead code — every function is called, every static is used.

## AC
- [ ] Remove dead functions from shim.rs (session_start_cmd, session_close_cmd, context_cache_cmd, health_hourly_cmd, health_daily_cmd, log_rotate_cmd, cruft_scan_cmd, fetch_memory_context, check_handoffs, chrono_parse_approx, chrono_lite_ts, decode_chunked)
- [ ] Remove guarded_hook/guarded_hook_sync from main.rs
- [ ] Remove unused functions from types.rs (permission_deny_json, decision_block_json)
- [ ] Remove delivery_mode from nudge.rs
- [ ] Remove role_pattern from process.rs
- [ ] Remove unused regex statics from search_hierarchy
- [ ] Remove unused state.rs methods
- [ ] shim.rs under 800 lines after cleanup
- [ ] Zero dead_code compiler warnings (excluding pre-existing search_hierarchy test failures)
- [ ] All tests pass

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/shim.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/main.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/types.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/nudge.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/process.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/state.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/search_hierarchy.rs

tasks.title: Fix AppleScript single-quote escaping in process.rs and git detection heuristic in infra_guardrails.rs
tasks.description:
## Problem
Two active bugs found in code review: (1) chorus-inject AppleScript payload breaks on single quotes — nudge text with apostrophes causes syntax error (-2741). Confirmed live: Kade's feedback nudge failed on "doesn't". (2) infra_guardrails git detection heuristic at line 170 assumes team repo if no cd command — wrong when Claude Code runs from any directory.

## Experience
I nudge a message with an apostrophe and it delivers. I run a git command from a non-team directory and the guardrail doesn't block it.

## AC
- [ ] Single quotes in nudge text properly escaped in AppleScript payload
- [ ] Nudge with apostrophe delivers via inject (not just queue)
- [ ] Git detection checks actual CWD, not absence of cd command
- [ ] Tests cover both fixes

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/inject.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/infra_guardrails.rs

tasks.title: Split shim.rs into command submodules — 1776 lines to src/commands/
tasks.description:
## Problem
shim.rs is 1776 lines with 22+ commands — session management, health checks, context cache, log rotation, nudge delivery, database init all in one file. Role-to-directory mapping duplicated 3 times. Unmaintainable — every edit risks breaking unrelated functionality.

## Experience
I open shim.rs to fix a nudge bug and I only see nudge code. Each command lives in its own file under src/commands/. Changes are isolated, testable, and reviewable.

## AC
- [ ] Create src/commands/ directory with submodules for major command groups
- [ ] Session commands (start, close) in commands/session.rs
- [ ] Health/ops commands in commands/health.rs
- [ ] Context cache in commands/context_cache.rs
- [ ] shim.rs reduced to dispatch + shared utilities (< 400 lines)
- [ ] Role-to-dir mapping extracted to one location (shared/state_paths.rs already has role_dir)
- [ ] All existing tests pass after refactor

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/shim.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/commands/mod.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/commands/session.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/commands/health.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/commands/context_cache.rs

tasks.title: Extract shared hook utilities — file_classification.rs, session_scanning.rs, state_paths.rs
tasks.description:
## Problem
Code review found: is_code_file() defined 4 times with different extension lists, session JSONL scanning in 6 hooks with varied patterns, /Users/jeffbridwell and /tmp paths hardcoded 20+ times. Every hook author copies logic instead of importing it. Bugs get fixed in one copy but not the others.

## Experience
I add a new file type exemption and it works everywhere — one change, one place. Hardcoded paths come from constants, not scattered string literals. Session scanning follows one pattern across all hooks.

## AC
- [ ] file_classification.rs: single is_code_file() with complete extension list, used by pair_gate, tdd_gate, log_first_gate, memory_gate
- [ ] session_scanning.rs: shared session JSONL reader, used by 6+ hooks
- [ ] state_paths.rs: constants for /tmp/claude-team-scan, /tmp/voice-inbox, chorus log path, repo root
- [ ] All 4 is_code_file call sites updated to use shared version
- [ ] Existing tests pass after refactor

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/shared/file_classification.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/shared/session_scanning.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/shared/state_paths.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/shared/mod.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/pair_gate.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/tdd_gate.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/log_first_gate.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/memory_gate.rs

tasks.title: Stable inject binary — extract osascript injection from chorus-hook-shim to avoid TCC permission revocation on rebuild
tasks.description:
## Problem
Every cargo build --release changes chorus-hook-shim binary hash. macOS TCC revokes both Accessibility AND Full Disk Access permissions. Jeff must manually toggle both off/on in System Settings after every rebuild. This session had 3 rebuilds = 6 potential manual toggles. Overnight, alerts couldn't wake roles because permissions were silently revoked.

## Experience
I rebuild chorus-hooks and nudge injection keeps working. No manual permission toggling needed. The privileged operations use a stable binary that never changes during normal development.

## AC
- [ ] Extract osascript injection into chorus-inject binary (tiny, stable, single-purpose)
- [ ] chorus-hook-shim delegates to chorus-inject for keystroke injection
- [ ] Only chorus-inject needs Accessibility permission — chorus-hook-shim doesn't
- [ ] Full Disk Access operations similarly isolated if needed
- [ ] cargo build --release of chorus-hooks does NOT invalidate any TCC permissions
- [ ] Test: rebuild chorus-hook-shim, verify nudge inject still works without toggling

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/process.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/inject.rs (new)
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/Cargo.toml

tasks.title: Integrate sexuality-player into Gathering app — Express route with auth, shared layout, Bedroom proxy for media
tasks.description:
## Experience
Jeff opens Gathering on his phone or laptop, taps Sexuality in the nav. The player loads behind the same login as everything else. Photos and videos stream from Bedroom — no separate port to remember, no separate app to manage.

## AC
- [ ] /sexuality route in Gathering Express app, behind auth
- [ ] Player UI served as EJS view with Gathering layout/nav
- [ ] API endpoints proxied to Bedroom:8090 (photos, videos, playlists, media)
- [ ] Photo strip, resizable divider, and all current features preserved
- [ ] Works on mobile (WiFi to Gathering, Gathering proxies to Bedroom)
- [ ] Standalone sexuality-player on Bedroom still runs for direct access

## Files
- src/routes/sexuality.ts — new route
- src/views/sexuality.ejs — player view
- server.js — register route

tasks.title: Stale nudge warning — emit nudge.stale when nudges sit unacknowledged 10+ minutes
tasks.description:
## Experience
Nudges get drained but I can't tell if they sat in the queue for 30 minutes before anyone processed them. A nudge that ages 10+ minutes without acknowledgment is a coordination failure — the sender is waiting and the recipient didn't notice. I want a stale nudge warning so I can see when the coordination loop is broken.

## AC
- [ ] Alert-runner checks nudge inbox files for age > 10 minutes
- [ ] Emit nudge.stale spine event with: recipient role, age in minutes, nudge content preview
- [ ] Warning appears in chorus.log for monitoring

## Files
- scripts/alert-runner.sh
- alerting/nudge-stale.yml

tasks.title: Clearing routes un-addressed messages to all roles — should go to Wren only per hint text
Pair gate blocks non-code files — CSS, HTML docs, static assets shouldn't require /pair
tasks.description:
## Problem
The pair gate hook requires /pair for cross-domain file edits, but it triggers on CSS, HTML docs, and static assets that don't need pair coordination. Editing clearing/public/index.html or a stylesheet shouldn't require loading the full pair protocol.

## Experience
I edit a CSS file or static HTML page and the build isn't blocked by a pair gate. The gate only fires for source code files where cross-domain risk is real.

## AC
- [ ] CSS files (.css) exempt from pair gate
- [ ] Static HTML files in public/ directories exempt from pair gate
- [ ] Source code files (.ts, .rs, .py, .sh) still trigger pair gate when cross-domain
- [ ] Test covers exempted file types

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/pair_gate.rs

tasks.title: Mobile-friendly doc rendering — shared responsive CSS for all gathering-docs and chorus-docs
tasks.description:
AC: 1. Shared responsive stylesheet (e.g. doc-responsive.css) added to public/. 2. All gathering-docs and chorus-docs HTML files include the stylesheet. 3. On mobile (max-width 768px): headings scale down, tables scroll horizontally, code blocks don't overflow, body text is readable without pinch-zoom. 4. Viewport meta tag present in all docs. 5. Jeff verifies on phone — doc-catalog child pages render cleanly.

## Experience
I open doc-catalog on my phone from the couch and tap into a service design doc. The text is readable without pinch-zooming, tables scroll instead of running off the edge, headings fit the screen. I can actually read my own architecture docs on mobile without fighting the layout.

## Blast Radius
- public/css/doc-responsive.css (new)
- public/gathering-docs/*.html
- public/chorus-docs/*.html
- public/doc-catalog.html

tasks.title: Post-build hook: re-register chorus-hook-shim Accessibility after cargo rebuild
tasks.description:
## Problem
Every `cargo build --release` of chorus-hooks changes the binary hash of chorus-hook-shim. macOS invalidates the Accessibility permission for the old hash, so osascript keystroke injection silently fails until Jeff manually toggles the permission off/on in System Settings.

## Experience
I rebuild chorus-hooks and nudges keep working — no manual step needed. The post-build script handles Accessibility re-registration automatically, or at minimum warns me that the permission needs re-toggling.

## AC
- [ ] After `cargo build --release`, chorus-hook-shim retains or re-acquires Accessibility permission
- [ ] Nudge --force inject works immediately after rebuild without manual intervention
- [ ] If auto-registration is not possible (SIP blocks TCC writes), emit a clear warning with the exact fix step

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/build.rs
- scripts/post-build-hook.sh

tasks.title: role-state preserves stale card ID on non-building transitions — waiting/idle should clear card field
tasks.description:
## Problem
When `role-state` transitions to `waiting` or `idle`, the `card=` field from the previous `building` state persists. Clearing tiles show stale card IDs for roles that have finished their work.

## Root Cause
The `role-state` Rust binary only sets `card=` when explicitly passed. Transitions like `role-state silas waiting` preserve the previous card value instead of clearing it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `role-state <role> waiting` clears the card field (sets to null/empty)
- [ ] `role-state <role> idle` clears the card field
- [ ] `role-state <role> building card=<id>` still sets the card field correctly
- [ ] Clearing tiles show no card ID for waiting/idle roles

## Experience
Jeff sees the Clearing tiles and each role shows its actual state — building with a card number, or waiting/idle with no card. No stale card IDs lingering from previous work.

tasks.title: WiFi access to doc-catalog — extensionless route, gathering-docs accessible after WiFi login
tasks.description: AC: 1. /doc-catalog serves the page without .html extension. 2. Doc-catalog and all gathering-docs children load over WiFi (192.168.86.36:3000) after login. 3. No auth changes — login still required for admin pages.

tasks.title: Clearing domain badge counts ignore role filter — header shows all-role total while sub-groups show filtered cards
tasks.description:
## Problem
When a role filter is active on the Clearing domain panel, the header badge shows unfiltered counts (e.g. 40 CARDS · 40 TOTAL) while sub-groups only render the filtered role's cards (e.g. 7). The numbers don't match.

## Root Cause
`clearing/public/index.html:1192-1194` uses `counts.activeCards` from the API (unfiltered) even when `activeRoleFilter !== 'all'` has already filtered the `cards` array.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] When role filter is active, domain header badge reflects filtered card count
- [ ] When role filter is 'all', badge shows full API counts as before
- [ ] Sub-group counts sum to domain header count

## Experience
Jeff sees matching numbers between domain header and sub-group totals regardless of filter state.

tasks.title: Infra guardrail false positive — blocks nudges discussing signal-0 liveness checks
tasks.description:
## Problem
The infra guardrail hook blocks nudge messages that discuss signal-0 liveness checks (e.g. mentioning `kill -0` or process signals). The hook pattern-matches infrastructure keywords too broadly, catching legitimate coordination messages.

## Experience
I nudge another role about a liveness check design and it goes through without being blocked. The guardrail catches actual infra mutations, not conversations about infra patterns.

## AC
- [ ] Nudge messages discussing signal-0 / kill -0 / liveness checks are not blocked
- [ ] Actual infra mutation commands (kill, rm, shutdown) remain blocked
- [ ] Test covers the false positive case

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/infra_guardrails.rs

tasks.title: Clearing mobile UX — Enter sends instead of newline, focus stolen by re-renders
tasks.description:
## Experience
Jeff is typing in the Clearing on his phone. Enter sends the message instead of adding a newline — messages get cut off mid-thought. Focus also gets stolen, probably by tile refresh or Socket.IO event re-render. Jeff can't compose a multi-line message on mobile.

## AC
1. Shift+Enter or mobile-friendly mechanism for newlines on phone
2. Focus not stolen from input field during tile/message refreshes
3. Send only on explicit Send button tap on mobile (Enter = newline)
4. Tests cover: mobile input retains focus after refresh, newline insertion works

tasks.title: App repo dirty file accumulation — cross-role changes block git rebase for every role
tasks.description:
## Experience
I ship a card and try to push. Git says 'cannot pull with rebase: unstaged changes.' The unstaged changes aren't mine — they're from other roles' prior work that nobody committed. Every role hits this wall. Silas spent 10 minutes just getting past it on #1904.

## AC
- [ ] Commit all current dirty files in the app repo as a sync commit
- [ ] Verify git status is clean after
- [ ] Push succeeds without rebase conflicts

## Files
- jeff-bridwell-personal-site (app repo — multiple dirty files)

tasks.title: Log-first gate fires on lint/build errors — should only trigger for runtime bugs
tasks.description:
## Experience
I fix a lint error — ESLint says NodeJS.Timeout is undefined. I change the type annotation. The log-first gate blocks me: 'check the logs first.' There are no runtime logs for a lint error. I have to fabricate a log statement to satisfy the gate before I can save a one-line type fix.

## AC
- [ ] Log-first gate skips when the edit is fixing a lint/build error (not a runtime bug)
- [ ] Runtime bug fixes still require log inspection (no regression)
- [ ] Test: lint fix edit passes gate, runtime fix edit requires logs

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/log_first_gate.rs

tasks.title: Context synthesis gate fires on new files with no git history — false positive blocks every new test file
tasks.description:
## Experience
I write a new test file, save it, and get blocked: 'Context synthesis gate: no git history on file.test.ts. Run git log first.' But the file is new — there IS no git history. I have to run a meaningless git log that returns nothing, then retry. Every new test file hits this wall.

## AC
- [ ] Context synthesis gate skips files with no git history (git log returns empty)
- [ ] Files with git history still trigger the gate (no regression)
- [ ] Test: new file write passes, existing file write requires git log

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/memory_gate.rs

tasks.title: Alerts must inject to role terminal via nudge --force, not just Bridge POST
tasks.description:
## Experience
Cloudflare tunnel went down. Alert fired to Bridge — Jeff saw it on his phone. But Silas was idle and never saw it in his terminal. The fix command was right there in the alert text but Silas couldn't act because it never reached him.

## AC
1. All alerts (tunnel, pipeline, disk, service health) use nudge --force to inject into the owning role's terminal
2. Alert still posts to Bridge for Jeff visibility (DEC-107: persist AND deliver)
3. Role receiving alert can act on it in the same terminal session
4. Tests cover: alert fires, nudge delivered to role terminal, Bridge also receives

## Files
- scripts/alert-runner.sh
- alerting/tunnel-down.yml
- alerting/seed-write-failure.yml
- alerting/app-down.yml
- alerting/hook-server-down.yml
- alerting/startup-sync-failure.yml

tasks.title: Clearing shows connected when tunnel is down — stale Socket.IO connection state on 5G
tasks.description:
## Experience
Jeff is at the pharmacy on 5G, connecting via clearing.lightlifeurbangardens.com. The Clearing shows 'connected' but messages don't land. Cloudflare tunnel was down. Socket.IO held a stale connected state instead of showing disconnected.

## AC
1. Socket.IO client detects tunnel/proxy failure and shows disconnected within 10 seconds
2. Heartbeat ping to server — if no pong within timeout, status switches to disconnected
3. Reconnect indicator shows when re-establishing after tunnel recovery
4. Tests cover: simulated tunnel drop shows disconnected, recovery shows reconnecting then connected

## Files
- chorus/directing/clearing/public/index.html (Socket.IO client connection handling)

tasks.title: Clearing visibility too restrictive — Jeff's terminal input and role thinking/ideation filtered out
tasks.description:
## Experience
Jeff types in Kade or Silas terminal — invisible in Clearing. Silas thinks and ideates — invisible in Clearing. The router whitelist (router.ts) only shows: jeff-input, demos, blocked, decisions, gemba, accept-requests. Everything else falls to hidden default. The Clearing is a conversation, not a filtered event stream. Participants should see each other.

## AC
1. Session tailer identifies Jeff's input and tags it as visible
2. Role thinking and ideation (non-coordination) visible in message stream
3. Role-to-role coordination noise (nudge delivery confirmations, state changes, tool calls) stays hidden
4. The distinction is: human-readable commentary visible, machine protocol hidden
5. Consistent experience across all surfaces — phone on public wifi, laptop in library, any device. Device identity changes, visibility behavior does not
6. Tests cover: Jeff terminal input visible, role commentary visible, nudge plumbing hidden, multi-device identity parity

tasks.title: Clearing streams pane only fills half the screen on mobile
tasks.description:
## Experience
I open the Clearing on my phone and the streams pane — where I see role messages and team activity — only fills half the viewport. The bottom half is empty space. On desktop it's fine. On mobile, that wasted space means I'm scrolling more to see less.

## AC
- [ ] Streams pane fills available viewport height on mobile (<768px)
- [ ] No layout regression on desktop
- [ ] Test: verify CSS media query targets mobile viewport

## Files
- chorus/directing/clearing/public/index.html (CSS section)

tasks.title: Watchdog false alerts — reports stale cards already accepted, wrong card assignments for roles
tasks.description:
## Experience
Jeff sees watchdog alerts saying Kade is unresponsive on #1718 (not his card) and Silas unresponsive on #2031 (already accepted). The alerts are noise — they erode trust in the system. Jeff should never see a false alert.

## AC
1. Watchdog reads current role-state declared JSON, not stale card assignments
2. Watchdog checks card status before alerting — no alerts on Done/accepted cards
3. State emission gap between acp and next card pull does not trigger false alert
4. Tests cover: accepted card no-alert, wrong-card no-alert, real stall fires alert

## Files
- platform/scripts/watchdog.sh

tasks.title: Cards CLI single-pass validation — all fields validated at once, infer defaults from context
tasks.description:
Cards CLI single-pass validation with inference — stop the 5-retry card creation loop.

AC:
1. `cards add` validates ALL required fields in one pass and reports every missing field in a single error message, not one at a time.
2. Inference: sequence inferred from domain when unambiguous (e.g. domain:chorus + context → sequence:framework). Chunk inferred from domain. Type inferred from title verb (fix→fix, add→new, update→enhance).
3. `--quick` bypasses desc/experience/blast-radius gates for unplanned work (already exists, verify it works).
4. Experience section warning remains a warning, not a blocker — it's a PM concern, not a creation gate.
5. Zero sequential gate failures on a well-formed `cards add` call.

## Experience
I tell a role to card something and they do it in one command, not five retries. The CLI helps them get it right, not punishes them for missing fields one at a time.

## Blast Radius
- platform/board-client/src/cli.ts
- platform/board-client/src/sdk.ts

tasks.title: Team-scan staleness detection — consume role.state.changed events, flag roles with no state transition in 45min
tasks.description:
team-scan shows role activity but can't tell Jeff if a role has gone dark. A role that hasn't changed state in 45 minutes could be stuck, crashed, or just thinking — but Jeff can't tell without checking each screen.

## Experience
Jeff glances at team-scan and sees [STALE] next to Kade. He knows immediately: Kade hasn't transitioned state in 45 minutes. Something needs attention. No screen-checking required.

## AC
- [ ] team-scan reads last role.state.changed timestamp from chorus.log for each role
- [ ] If no state change in 45min, scan output includes STALE flag next to role
- [ ] Threshold configurable
- [ ] Tests cover fresh, stale, and missing-event cases

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/observer.rs
- platform/scripts/session-start-thin.sh

tasks.title: Cards CLI completeness — fix set command, add label removal, bulk ops, creation validation, eliminate Vikunja fallback
tasks.description:
## Experience
Jeff uses the Clearing domain panel and cards CLI for all board interaction. Vikunja UI is not used. But CLI gaps force fallback to Vikunja or create silent data corruption.

## Files
- platform/board-client/src/sdk.ts
- platform/board-client/src/cli.ts

## AC
- [ ] `cards set` produces prefixed labels (sequence:X not bare X) — matches sequence-tag behavior
- [ ] `cards tag` either works or gives a clear error pointing to the correct command
- [ ] `cards untag <id> <label>` removes a specific label from a card
- [ ] `cards bulk-move <ids> <status>` moves multiple cards at once
- [ ] `cards add` requires --sequence or warns (soft gate, not hard block)
- [ ] `cards list` output shows sequence: prefix on sequence labels for greppability
- [ ] All 6 items verified with tests

tasks.title: Board data quality audit — duplicate labels, missing sequences, orphan tags, position integrity
tasks.description:
## Experience
I look at the board in the Clearing and the numbers don't add up. Sub-groups show 5 cards but the header says 46. Cards created during fast ops sessions have bare labels, missing sequences, or wrong tags. The board is team memory — if the tags are wrong, the team's awareness of its own work is wrong.

## Files
- platform/board-client/src/sdk.ts
- platform/board-client/src/cli.ts

## AC
- [ ] Audit all cards for bare labels (not prefixed with chunk:/domain:/type:/sequence:) — report count
- [ ] Remove or fix bare labels using cards untag/set
- [ ] Verify all active cards (WIP/Next/Later) have: domain, chunk, type, sequence
- [ ] Report cards missing required tags — fix or flag
- [ ] Verify no duplicate labels on any card
- [ ] Verify Done/Won't Do cards are excluded from active counts in Clearing domain panel

tasks.title: L2 Awareness service design — as-is inventory of compound loop, ops awareness, gemba, alerts, health
tasks.description:
The team keeps being surprised by what's already built. L2 (Awareness) has shipped components scattered across hooks, scripts, alerts, and agents with no single view.

## Experience
Jeff opens the Awareness domain page and sees everything: docs, UIs, APIs, data, tests, services. One page, one answer. No more 'do we have this?' — it's all there.

## AC
- [ ] Domain page rendered as HTML with sections: Docs, UIs, APIs, Data, Tests, Services
- [ ] Each section lists concrete artifacts with file paths and descriptions
- [ ] References protocol-stack.html L2 section as the architecture doc
- [ ] Shows forcing functions, not just components

## Files
- designing/domain-context/domain-context-awareness.html
- product-manager/artifacts/awareness-service-design.html

tasks.title: Gemba blind spot on reboot — session indexer doesn't detect new sessions for minutes
tasks.description:
Session indexer (chorus-watch-sessions.sh via fswatch) doesn't detect new sessions for 4+ minutes after a role reboots. Jeff watched Kade reboot and the gemba showed stale data the entire time. This makes observation useless during the most critical moment — when a role comes back up.

## Experience
Jeff reboots a role and watches gemba. He should see the role boot, read its session-start file, and start working — in real time. Instead: 4 minutes of stale data from the previous session. Jeff is blind during the exact moment he most needs visibility.

## AC
- [ ] gemba-tick shows new session activity within 30 seconds of reboot
- [ ] gemba-tick.sh reads session JSONL directly instead of depending on the index
- [ ] Works for all three roles (wren, silas, kade)
- [ ] No regression on indexed search — direct tail is supplementary, not replacement

## Files
- platform/scripts/gemba-tick.sh
- platform/scripts/gemba-start.sh

tasks.title: Log reclassification phase 3 — 13 warn-to-error upgrades across middleware, seed processing, and query failures
tasks.description:
Audit found 40+ warn-level logs that describe actual errors. Phase 3 covers the 13 highest-impact: auth/security rejections (7), seed media processing failures (4), content parse failures (2). Plus 2 error-to-warn downgrades for false errors.

## Experience
When Jeff checks Loki for errors, he sees the real count — not a filtered subset hiding behind warn level. Every failure that affects what he sees is classified as error. No more hunting through warnings to find actual problems.

## AC
- [ ] Upgrade to error: twilio-auth (sig validation), webhook-auth, csrf, cors, pod-storage (4 patterns), service-token
- [ ] Upgrade to error: seed-pod.service (video transcode, WAV conversion, whisper transcription, audio extraction)
- [ ] Upgrade to error: collection.handler (blog parse), docs.handler (slug validation)
- [ ] Downgrade to warn: SemanticSearchService init, Fuseki retry
- [ ] Loki error count reflects reality after reclassification

## Files
- src/middleware/twilio-auth.middleware.ts
- src/middleware/pod-storage.middleware.ts
- src/middleware/csrf.middleware.ts
- src/middleware/cors.middleware.ts
- src/services/seed-pod.service.ts
- src/handlers/collection.handler.ts
- src/handlers/docs.handler.ts
- src/services/fuseki-sync.service.ts

tasks.title: Index domain crawler snapshots into Chorus search — periodic crawl writes to SQLite/LanceDB for compound loop discovery
tasks.description:
## Experience
Jeff says 'seeds are broken' and the compound loop automatically surfaces not just prior conversations about seeds, but today's Loki errors, the alert rules watching it, and the code files that implement it. Operational context compounds alongside conversation context — no manual crawl needed.

## AC
- [ ] Periodic crawler runs on schedule (LaunchAgent or cron) for each active domain
- [ ] Crawler results written as indexed messages into Chorus SQLite — same format as session messages
- [ ] Hybrid search returns crawler snapshots alongside conversation results
- [ ] Snapshot freshness: code/alerts refresh daily, logs refresh hourly
- [ ] Existing compound loop (context_inject.rs) finds crawler data with zero code changes

## Files
- platform/api/src/server.ts (crawler endpoint — source data)
- platform/scripts/index-crawler-snapshots.sh (new — periodic indexer)
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/context_inject.rs (verify — no changes needed)

tasks.title: Board domain total doesn't match sub-chunk sum — Chorus shows 46 total but sub-groups add to 5
tasks.description:
## Experience
I open the Clearing domain panel and see 'Chorus: 46 cards' at the top. I expand the sub-groups and count: 5 cards total across all groups. The total doesn't match the parts. Either the total counts cards the groups don't show, or the grouping logic is dropping cards.

## AC
- [ ] Diagnose: identify which cards are counted in the total but missing from sub-groups
- [ ] Fix: domain total matches the sum of sub-group counts
- [ ] Test: verify total === sum of sub-groups for all domains with sub-chunks

## Files
- src/handlers/team.handler.ts (or wherever Clearing domain panel data is served)
- platform/board-client/src/cli.ts (sequence/chunk display)

tasks.title: Bad URI graph load errors — 1294 data quality issues in people/connections TTL filenames
tasks.description:
## Experience
Jeff queries Loki for errors and sees 1294 bad URI graph load failures from people/connections TTL files. The filenames contain characters that break URI parsing. These aren't app bugs — they're data quality issues in the source TTL files that need cleanup.

## AC
- [ ] Identify which TTL files in people/connections produce bad URI errors
- [ ] Fix or regenerate the offending filenames to be URI-safe
- [ ] Verify Loki error count drops to zero for bad URI graph loads after fix
- [ ] No data loss — all people/connections records preserved

## Files
- data/people/ (TTL files with bad URIs)
- src/services/fuseki-sync.service.ts (loads the TTL files)

tasks.title: Cloudflare tunnel monitoring — alert when tunnel dies, seeds silently stop
tasks.description:
The Cloudflare tunnel (lightlifeurbangardens.com → localhost:3000) is the public entry point for seeds. When it dies, SMS webhooks from Twilio can't reach the app. Seeds silently stop arriving — no error, no alert, no trace. Jeff discovers it by sending a seed and it not showing up.

Tunnel has failed silently at least twice (Feb 19, Mar 5). Each time Jeff was the monitoring.

## Experience
Jeff sends a seed. It arrives. Always. If the tunnel is down, Jeff knows before he sends — not after. The system tells him, not the other way around.

## AC
- [ ] Periodic health check on tunnel endpoint (curl the public URL or cloudflared /ready)
- [ ] Alert fires within 60 seconds of tunnel going down — notify Bridge immediately
- [ ] check-seeds.sh includes tunnel status in its health output (already does tunnel=200)
- [ ] Ops awareness gate (#2003) surfaces tunnel-down state on every prompt

## Files
- alerting/tunnel-down.yml
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/ops_awareness.rs
- platform/scripts/check-seeds.sh

tasks.title: Ollama unreachable on Bedroom — hybrid search degrades to FTS-only, no semantic vectors
tasks.description:
Ollama on Bedroom (192.168.86.242:11434) is unreachable. The Chorus API hybrid search silently degrades to FTS-only — no semantic vectors. The compound loop (#2003) works but without the richness Jeff asked for.

## Experience
When the compound loop fires, Jeff should get the richest possible context — semantic similarity finding connections that keyword search misses. Right now it's keyword-only because Ollama is down on Bedroom. Fix it and the search gets smarter.

## AC
- [ ] Ollama service running on Bedroom (192.168.86.242:11434)
- [ ] Chorus API hybrid search returns semantic results (mode: hybrid in response, not just fts)
- [ ] Health endpoint shows vectors > 0 and hooks: active

## Files
- SSH to Bedroom: check Ollama service status
- platform/api/src/server.ts (OLLAMA_URL env var)

tasks.title: Operational deploy governance — runbook, pre/post checks, rollback discipline
tasks.description:
Deploys are ungoverned. Roles run app-state.sh deploy without pre-checks, post-verification, or rollback plans. Today's seed failures came from deploys that broke write paths while reads passed. No deploy checklist, no smoke test after deploy, no ownership of deploy verification.

## Experience
When a role deploys, Jeff should never discover the breakage. The deploy process itself should catch it — pre-deploy smoke, post-deploy verification, automatic rollback if health degrades. One command, one runbook, zero surprises.

## AC
- [ ] Operational runbook document covering: deploy sequence, pre-checks, post-verification, rollback
- [ ] Pre-deploy gate: smoke tests must pass before app-state.sh deploy proceeds
- [ ] Post-deploy verification: health + functional checks run automatically after deploy
- [ ] Rollback procedure documented and tested
- [ ] All roles follow the same deploy process — no ad-hoc deploys

tasks.title: Chorus API health endpoint — /api/chorus/health for monitoring
tasks.description:
Chorus API has no health endpoint — probes and agent-state.sh can only check PID, not functional status.

## Experience
When Jeff or a role checks if the API is healthy, they should get real answers: is the database reachable, are vectors loaded, is the hook server injecting context. Not just 'process has a PID.'

## AC
- [ ] GET /api/chorus/health returns JSON with db status, uptime, vector count
- [ ] Probes (seed-probe, clearing-probe) can use it as liveness check
- [ ] Returns 503 if database unreachable
- [ ] Hook server functional status — confirms context injection is active, not just PID alive (#2008 connection, per Wren feedback on #2009)

## Files
- platform/api/src/server.ts

tasks.title: Deduplicate LaunchAgent namespaces — consolidate com.chorus and com.gathering
tasks.description:
53 LaunchAgents across two namespaces — com.chorus.* and com.gathering.* — with duplicates doing the same job (context-cache, cruft-scan, perf-baseline). Confusing, wasteful, and makes agent-state.sh health report misleading.

## Experience
When Jeff runs agent-state.sh health, every agent should be there once with a clear name. Not two context-cache agents in different namespaces both firing on the same schedule. One namespace per job, no confusion.

## AC
- [ ] Audit all 53 agents — map which are duplicated across namespaces
- [ ] Retire com.gathering.* duplicates (context-cache, cruft-scan, perf-baseline) — one namespace owns each job
- [ ] Remove retired plists from ~/Library/LaunchAgents/
- [ ] Verify no double-firing after consolidation

## Files
- ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.gathering.context-cache-*.plist
- ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.gathering.cruft-scan.plist
- ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.gathering.perf-baseline.plist

tasks.title: LaunchAgent lifecycle tool — app-state.sh for native services
tasks.description:
LaunchAgent lifecycle is currently manual — roles kill PIDs, forget to use launchctl, leave orphans holding ports. Jeff has said repeatedly: no raw process killing. app-state.sh handles Docker containers but native LaunchAgents have no equivalent tool.

## Experience
When Jeff reboots a role or restarts a service, it should be one command that just works — like app-state.sh does for Docker. Today roles guess at launchctl syntax, leave orphan processes, and Jeff discovers hung ports. The experience: agent-state.sh restart chorus-hooks and it's done. No orphans, no port conflicts, no manual PID hunting.

## AC
- [ ] Script manages LaunchAgent start/stop/restart/status for all com.chorus.* and com.gathering.* agents
- [ ] Detects and kills orphan processes holding ports (ppid=1, not managed by launchd)
- [ ] Single command: agent-state.sh {start|stop|restart|status|orphans} [service-name]
- [ ] Hook updated to allow agent-state.sh as approved kill path

## Files
- platform/scripts/agent-state.sh
- platform/scripts/test-agent-state.sh
- platform/tests/features/gates/agent_lifecycle.feature

tasks.title: Compound loop breaks on reboot — after-prompt context injection lost on session boundary
tasks.description:
After-prompt hooks built April 3 search Chorus, logs, cards on every prompt. Worked. Jeff rebooted Wren, context injection stopped. Hook server running but loop broken.

## Experience
When Jeff reboots a role, the compound loop should just keep working. The role boots, Jeff types something, and the after-prompt hook fires — searching Chorus, tailing logs, checking cards. No manual restart. No lost context. The loop survives the session boundary.

## AC
- [ ] After-prompt hooks fire on first prompt of new session — verified by log evidence
- [ ] Chorus/logs/cards results injected into role context every prompt
- [ ] Reboot test: restart role, send prompt, verify injected context appears
- [ ] No session starts without compound loop active

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/context_inject.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/main.rs
- platform/scripts/test-compound-loop.sh

tasks.title: /cs shows routing tag instead of seed content — photo seed displayed as '#wren' instead of showing what Jeff actually sent
tasks.description:
## Experience
Jeff sends a photo seed tagged #wren. On next /cs or session start, the receiving role reads the photo and describes what's in it. 'Birdbath with verdigris patina, light through water, leaves at the bottom — your garden.' Jeff knows his seed arrived because someone received it, looked at it, and responded to it. Like a person would.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] /cs shows seed content: text for text seeds, URL for links, description for photos — not hashtags, not file paths
- [ ] Photo seeds: the role reads the image file and generates a human description of what's in it
- [ ] The description appears in /cs output so Jeff sees it without asking
- [ ] Jeff sends a photo from his phone, runs /cs, sees a description of what he sent — verified live

## Files
- platform/scripts/check-seeds.sh
- src/adapters/sms-seed.adapter.ts

tasks.title: Log level reclassification phase 1 — Fuseki retries, event loop lag, health failures

Jeff queries Loki for errors and sees the real failure count — not 328 but 11,000+.

## Acceptance Criteria

tasks.title: Log level reclassification phase 2 — chorus-events, foaf prefix, bad URI graph loads

tasks.title: Four-layer compound search — Chorus + logs + git + code on every search, all errors not warns
tasks.description:
## Experience
Every search I run gets context from all four layers automatically: (1) Chorus — what has the team said about this, (2) Logs — what is the system reporting right now, (3) Git — what changed and when, (4) Code — what currently exists. Each layer compounds on the others. All must run. Every time. No exceptions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search gate runs all four layers on every Grep/Glob: Chorus query, log check, git history, then allow the code search
- [ ] Each layer's results are injected into the deny message — the role sees all four before retrying
- [ ] All failures are errors, not warns — dropped seeds, missing context, stale state
- [ ] Retry after deny goes through clean (existing session-scoped dedupe)
- [ ] Sub-500ms total for all four layers (Chorus SQLite, log tail, git log are all local)
- [ ] Tests verify all four layers appear in deny message content

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/search_hierarchy.rs
- platform/tests/features/gates/memory_first.feature

tasks.title: Continuous awareness gate — surface unresolved state on every prompt cycle, not just boot
tasks.description:
## Experience
Every prompt cycle, the role sees active system state AND relevant context from prior sessions. Semantic + keyword search runs automatically against the Chorus index on every prompt. No role has to remember to check — the system brings the context to them. The compound loop: every interaction makes the system smarter.

## AC
- [ ] UserPromptSubmit hook extracts terms from Jeff's message, runs semantic + keyword search against Chorus index
- [ ] Results injected into role context automatically — role sees relevant prior discussions, decisions, and patterns
- [ ] PostToolUse hook checks active state on every tool call (ops alerts, pending seeds, pipeline health, unresolved flags)
- [ ] Surfaces warnings via stderr injection — not blocking, surfacing
- [ ] State checks are fast (sub-100ms) — cached, not live API calls on every prompt
- [ ] Tests: semantic search returns relevant results for known topics; keyword search returns exact matches; both inject on every prompt
- [ ] Replaces boot-only triage — this IS the compound loop closer

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/context_inject.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/main.rs
- platform/scripts/test-continuous-awareness.sh

tasks.title: Remove brief-writing from seed handler — seeds live in Fuseki, visible via /cs, briefs are a redundant broken path
tasks.description:

Jeff sends a seed via SMS. It appears in Fuseki and is visible through /cs. No brief file is created anywhere. One path, one store.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Delete writeBriefs() from seed.handler.ts
- [ ] Delete all brief-writing code paths from seed capture/routing
- [ ] Verify /cs still shows seeds correctly after removal
- [ ] Verify no seed brief files are created when a real SMS arrives
- [ ] No test seeds create brief files either

tasks.title: Seed write failure alert — fire on SPARQL update error, notify Bridge immediately
tasks.description:

Jeff sends a seed. It fails silently. Nobody tells him. He finds out hours later when he checks.

- [ ] Alert posts to Bridge so Jeff sees it

Health checks that dont test writes are theater.

tasks.title: Fix /cs skill — must hit seeds API, show pending seeds, verify pipeline health
tasks.description:
## Experience
I say "check seeds" and get a real answer — how many pending, when the last one arrived, whether the pipeline is healthy. Not "inbox clear" while my texts evaporate.

## AC
- [ ] /cs hits the seeds API (localhost:3000/api/seeds or SPARQL) to get pending seed count
- [ ] Shows each pending seed: type, content preview, timestamp, hashtag

tasks.title: Photo detail shows thumbnail instead of full image
tasks.description:
## Experience
I click on a photo in the grid and get a blurry thumbnail instead of the full-size image. I want to see my photos — the detail view should show the real thing, not a tiny render scaled up.

## AC
1. Photo detail view serves full-resolution image, not thumbnail
2. Verify with 5+ photos across different sources (Apple, Takeout, iPhone)
3. If full image unavailable, show thumbnail with a clear indicator that full-size is missing

tasks.title: Name face clusters — Jeff reviews 48 clusters, system persists names to Person nodes
tasks.description:
## Experience
I open the face cluster browser and see 48 unnamed groups. I know these people — family, friends, neighbors. I click a cluster, see the sample photos, type 'Mom', and it sticks. The name flows through to every photo she's in. 48 clusters, one sitting, and suddenly my photos know who's in them.

## AC
- [ ] Face cluster review UI shows representative sample per cluster
- [ ] Jeff can assign a name, system writes to Person node
- [ ] Named clusters propagate to canonical photo enrichment (jb:depictsPerson)
- [ ] Progress tracking: N of 48 clusters named

tasks.title: Rebuild semantic embeddings after photo enrichment — richer metadata for search quality
tasks.description:
## Experience
I search for 'beach trip 2019' and get garbage. The embeddings were built when the metadata was thin — no album names, no person names, no locations. Now that we've enriched the photos, search should actually work. I type a natural query and get the photos I'm thinking of.

## AC
- [ ] Embeddings rebuilt after #1627 album import completes
- [ ] Content strings include album names, person names, location, descriptions
- [ ] Semantic search returns relevant results for natural queries (person + time + place)
- [ ] Cross-domain links: photos ↔ stories (date overlap), photos ↔ people (face + name)

tasks.title: Stamp records with source-chain provenance — schema hashes + lineage + era-scoped authority
tasks.description:
## Experience
I look at a photo record and can't tell where it came from. Did this go through Picasa? Was it an iPhone original? Is the date from the camera or from a Google upload? I need to see the chain — camera → PC → Picasa → Google → export — and know which source is authoritative for this era.

## AC
- [ ] story-harvester stamps instanceSchemaHash + instanceMappingHash on every record
- [ ] notes-harvester stamps instanceSchemaHash + instanceMappingHash on every record
- [ ] photos harvester stamps instanceSchemaHash + instanceMappingHash on every record
- [ ] Each record carries icd:sourceChain — ordered list of systems it passed through
- [ ] Each record carries icd:authoritativeSource — era-scoped (which provider is golden for this time period)
- [ ] SPARQL query can find records produced under a specific hash pair
- [ ] SPARQL query can find records by authoritative source era
- [ ] icd-consistency-test.py --hashes verifies instance provenance matches current hashes

## Files
- src/services/story-harvester.service.ts
- src/services/notes-harvester.service.ts
- scripts/build-canonical-photos.py
- src/services/icd.service.ts (getVersionHashes call)
- scripts/icd-consistency-test.py
- src/ontology/icd-instance-photos.ttl (provider chain model)

tasks.title: iOS app for local wifi — native client for Gathering API on property/garden/photos
tasks.description:
## Experience
I'm walking the garden and see something — a new bloom, a bed that needs attention, damage from last night's storm. I pull out my phone and it's right there: my photos, my garden beds, my property notes. All on local wifi, no cloud, no login. I take a photo tagged to the bed I'm standing in front of. Later at my desk it's already in the system.

## AC
1. SwiftUI iOS app connects to Gathering API over local wifi (192.168.86.36:3000)
2. Browse photos natively — grid view, albums, face clusters
3. Garden walk mode — take photos tagged to garden beds/zones
4. Property inspection — view bed status, seasonal history, observations
5. Runs only on local network — no cloud auth, respects concentric trust
6. Uses existing API endpoints (DEC-093) — no new backend work needed

## Context
Jeff has 370 API endpoints on the Gathering app. An iOS app is a native client for that API, not a new system. Local wifi only — Library serves, iPhone consumes. Garden, photos, property are the first use cases.

tasks.title: Persist UX empathy research as product artifact — output budgets, phone gates, nudge timing, academic citations
tasks.description:
## Experience
I open a reference page and see the research on making agent teams actually helpful — not just productive. Output budgets, phone rendering gates, nudge timing, exception-only reporting, consolidated digests. Each one has a source citation and a buildability assessment. When a role starts a card that touches Jeff's experience, this page is the first thing they read.

## AC
1. HTML artifact at product-manager/artifacts/ux-empathy-research.html with all findings from 2026-04-01 research session
2. Organized by buildability: low/medium/high effort with impact ratings
3. Source citations linked (Fowler, Osmani, IBM, Amazon UXAgent, field studies)
4. Referenced from CLAUDE.md or doc-catalog so roles find it before building

## Files
- product-manager/artifacts/ux-empathy-research.html

tasks.title: Demo auto-screenshot — /demo captures screen before generating brief
tasks.description:
## Experience
I demo a card and the brief is a wall of text. Jeff wants to see the thing working, not read about it. The demo skill should auto-capture a screenshot of the relevant page before generating the brief — the image IS the demo evidence.

## AC
- [ ] /demo skill runs /lc (or role-screenshot.sh) before generating the brief
- [ ] Screenshot path included in the demo brief
- [ ] Demo brief is 2-3 sentences + screenshot, not paragraphs
- [ ] Builder can override with a specific URL to screenshot

## Files
- platform/scripts/demo-screenshot.sh (new)
- skills/demo (skill definition)

tasks.title: Chorus product visualization — DNA/Expression genome model for /chorus page
tasks.description:
## Experience
I open /chorus and see the team's genome — what persists across sessions (DNA: decisions, gates, skills, card types, pulse) and what it produces (Expression: Clearing, /werk, /loom, doc-catalog). Four actors inside. Click any node and drill into the detail. The narrative is biological: the team has DNA that survives reboots and expresses as behavior.

## AC
- [ ] HTML design artifact showing the genome visualization (inner ring DNA, outer ring Expression, actors inside)
- [ ] Node inventory: which decisions/gates/skills map to DNA, which surfaces map to Expression
- [ ] Drill-down spec: click DNA node → see components, click Expression node → see live surface
- [ ] Narrative connects to Shared Awareness five layers (Time, Pulse, Memory, Knowledge, Context)
- [ ] Design reviewed by Silas and Kade before build

## Design
- [/chorus page](/chorus) — existing visual to evolve
- [Shared Awareness drawing](/docs/pm/artifacts/actor-diagram-nudge.html)
- [Card Type Spec](/docs/pm/artifacts/card-type-spec.html)
- [Pulse Service Design](/docs/pm/artifacts/service-design-pulse.html)

## Files
- product-manager/artifacts/genome-visualization.html (new design artifact)

tasks.title: PM-driven tab sharing — Wren navigates screen sharing during demos
tasks.description:
## Experience
I'm on a demo call narrating what the team built, and I'm also fumbling with Chrome tabs to show the right page. I want Wren to drive the screen — I say "show convergence" and the tab switches. I focus on telling the story, the PM handles the stage. That's how a real demo team works.

## AC
1. Wren can switch which Chrome tab is shared during a Google Meet call
2. Demo flow script drives tab transitions — PM says 'show convergence' and the tab switches
3. Works with Jeff's existing Meet setup — no manual tab switching needed

## Why
Jeff narrates demos, PM should stage them. Currently Jeff manages both narration and screen mechanics. Separating those roles lets Jeff focus on the story while Wren manages the visual surface. Idea from 2026-03-26 demo prep.

tasks.title: Short video replay for team — capture 5-10 second clips for roles to see what Jeff sees
tasks.description:
## Experience
I see a bug on screen and I type 20 lines trying to describe it to the team. A 5-second screen clip would show it instantly. I want to drop a short video into the Bridge the same way I drop a screenshot — tap a button, capture what's happening, and the roles can see exactly what I see without me translating visual problems into text.

Jeff types 20 lines to describe what a 5-second clip shows instantly. Visual-to-text translation is real friction.

## AC
- [ ] Mechanism to capture short screen clips (5-10 sec) from Bridge or Claude
- [ ] Frame extraction so roles can see the interaction sequence
- [ ] Shareable artifact — drop a clip in Bridge like dropping an image
- [ ] Works from Jeff phone or desktop

tasks.title: Help Jeff simplify personal password management tooling
Borg two-phase delivery — hand drawings to HTML iteration, then build from validated prototype
tasks.description:
## What
Document and operationalize Borg's two-phase delivery pattern as a repeatable methodology.

## Phase 1: Iterate with client (spike/discovery)
- Hand drawings → HTML prototypes, rapidly iterated
- Refine UI + OWL + value stream + domains as we go
- 2-3 cycles
- Identify integrations, security, and harvest considerations along the way
- Output: validated prototype that captures intent

## Phase 2: Build from 1 (commitment)
- The iteration output becomes the spec — estimable, buildable
- Code + tests + UX + tickets + logs + monitoring + security + documentation
- Full blast radius coverage (DEC-072)

## Why
This is Jeff's natural working pattern — validated by the Athena experience at Staples. Phase 1 is discovery through making. Phase 2 is commitment informed by understanding. Maps to spike/discovery/commitment taxonomy.

## Athena lesson
Process weight kills adoption. Phase 1 must stay lightweight — sketches, conversation, rapid cycles. Phase 2 inherits rigor from the system, not from human paperwork.

## AC
- [ ] Pattern documented in About library
- [ ] Card lifecycle integrates the two phases (spike → discovery → commitment maps to Phase 1 → Phase 2)
- [ ] Borg can execute Phase 1 autonomously given hand drawings as input

## Tags
[medium] [discovery] chorus methodology

tasks.title: Album reviews — pull critic/user reviews into album detail page
Voice Analytics as Reflecting hub — aggregate posture, sentiment, session tempo, personal analytics
BDD now-gate pass
tasks.description:

tasks.title: Memory gate missing search-order enforcement — only gates Edit/Write, not Grep/Bash searches
tasks.description:
## Experience
I grep the filesystem without checking Chorus first. The memory gate doesn't block me because it only fires on Edit/Write (line 217 of memory_gate.rs). Grep and Bash searches pass through unchecked.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PreToolUse gate intercepts Grep and Bash search commands (grep, rg, find on session/card files)
- [ ] Gate blocks if no prior chorus-query.sh call detected in the session
- [ ] Gate allows if Chorus was already queried this session
- [ ] Tests cover: grep without Chorus (blocked), grep after Chorus (allowed), non-search Bash (allowed)
